description:
Audit existing FlowDoc documentation for drift and apply fixes, one item per 
loop/judge cycle. Spawns: root epic -> a single audit spawner (detect drift, 
score by impact, write _UpdateQueue.md) -> one bead per queue item created at 
runtime (UPDATE / NEW / REMOVE / VERIFY / LEAKAGE) -> a finalize bead gated on 
all of them that archives the queue into _AuditLog.md, then a validate bead 
(depends_on finalize) that scores the maintained docs against the authoring 
contract. Replaces the 
flowdoc-maintainer / flowdoc-user-maintainer agent-loop. Requires the matching 
DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md already exists (run flowdoc-generate first). Run via 
'bd mol pour flowdoc-maintain --var audience=maintainer|user'.

audience.description: maintainer = audit __docs/ (engineer impact scoring); user = audit _docs/ (end-user impact scoring + developer-leakage scan).

steps.title: FlowDoc: maintain {{audience}} documentation
steps.description:
Umbrella for a FlowDoc maintenance pass ({{audience}} edition).

AUDIENCE MAP:
  - maintainer -> audit __docs/ ; score by engineer/maintainer impact.
  - user       -> audit _docs/  ; score by end-user impact; also strip any
    developer content that leaked into user docs.

Three authored children: 'audit' (detect drift + spawn one fix bead per
finding), 'finalize' (archive the queue once every fix is closed, gated on
children-of(audit)), and 'validate' (depends_on finalize; score the maintained
docs against the authoring contract). Work the leaves, not the epic.
Precondition: DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md must already exist.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: audit for drift, then spawn one fix bead per finding
steps.description:
AUDIT + FAN-OUT (one bead). Your bead id is shown as "Complete beads issue
<id>" at the top of this goal — call it $SELF.

AUDIENCE = {{audience}}.  DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.
PRECONDITION: if DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md does not exist, stop and report that
flowdoc-generate must be poured first — do NOT spawn anything.

== Part A: detect drift (one pass) ==
1. Survey current code: list_files(".", recursive=True); read dependency
   manifests for version changes; note new / renamed / deleted files.
2. Load doc state: read DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md and each doc's Implementation
   Map (maintainer) / documented steps (user).
3. Run detection strategies:
   - Implementation Map check: do the files a doc cites still exist and match?
   - Dependency drift: current manifest vs Architecture.md tech-stack table.
   - Entry-point check: undocumented routes/commands/exports, and documented
     entry points that no longer exist.
   - Config drift: documented config keys vs actual config/.env.
   - (user edition ONLY) Developer-leakage scan of existing docs: source paths
     (src/, *.ts, *.py), shell/install commands (npm/git/make), internal URLs
     (localhost/127.0.0.1/staging/.dev).
4. Score each finding by impact and bucket it:
   - maintainer: entry/public-API change +10, core flow +8, new dep +5,
     config +5, helper +2, cosmetic 0.
   - user: documented step broken +10, UI moved +8, new required step +8,
     feature removed-but-documented +7, new capability undocumented +5,
     flag/option changed +5, config +4, leakage +4, error-text +2, wording +1.
     (user) M5 — BEFORE scoring, gate each change through "does this change
     what a user SEES or DOES?":
       COUNT it (queue it) if it adds/moves/renames a menu item, screen,
         button, field, icon, or label; changes a user-visible step,
         message, or required input; or adds/removes a user-facing
         capability.
       IGNORE it (do NOT queue — this stops the audit flagging noise) if it
         is an internal rename/refactor, a test-only change, a dependency
         bump with no UI change, a logging/perf/CI/build change, or any code
         edit with zero user-visible effect.
   Tiers: >=8 critical, 4-7 medium, 1-3 low, 0 verify-only.
5. Write DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md with this FIXED shape (M4) so downstream
   consumers can rely on it:
     - a one-line audit summary + counters (Total / Critical / Medium / Low /
       Verify) and a findings-by-category table;
     - the queue split into emoji-tagged BUCKET SECTIONS, each item carrying a
       STABLE ID PREFIX so the action is readable from the id:
         ## 🔴 Critical (UPDATE)   items "- [ ] U001 | [Name](Section/Name.md) | <what drifted>"
         ## 🆕 New (missing docs)  items "- [ ] N001 | [Name](Section/Name.md) | <what to add>"
         ## 🗑️ Remove (stale docs) items "- [ ] R001 | [Name](Section/Name.md) | <why remove>"
         ## 🚿 Leakage (user only)  items "- [ ] L001 | [Name](Section/Name.md) | <dev content to strip>"
         ## 🔹 Minor                items "- [ ] M001 | [Name](Section/Name.md) | <cosmetic>"
         ## ✅ Verify-only            items "- [ ] V001 | [Name](Section/Name.md) | <confirm current>"
       (Prefixes: U=update, N=new, R=remove, L=leakage, M=minor, V=verify.)
   Append a dated entry to DOCS_DIR/_AuditLog.md.

== Part B: fan out — ONE bead per queue item (deadlock-safe contract) ==
For EACH queue item create exactly one child bead:
  bd create --type=task --priority=<1 if critical, 2 if medium, 3 if low> --parent=$SELF --title='FlowDoc {{audience}} <ACTION>: <Name>' --description='<ACTION> the doc for audience={{audience}}, where <ACTION> is the queue id prefix (U/N/R/L/M/V):
       UPDATE (U/M) = re-read source and fix ONLY the sections the drift type
         implicates: Implementation Map if files moved/renamed; How It Works /
         System Perspective if behavior changed; Configuration if settings
         changed; Edge Cases / Error Scenarios if new gotchas; for Flow docs
         the Step-by-Step, Where (file:symbol), the mermaid diagram, and
         Failure Handling. Add a > [!NOTE] Last Updated: <date> stamp.
       NEW (N) = create the doc from the matching template in
         DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md (PascalCase filename) and add it to
         _Manifest.md with a banded id.
       REMOVE (R) = delete the doc, drop it from _Manifest.md and any index,
         fix inbound links.
       VERIFY (V) = re-read source and walk the VERIFY checklist; no rewrite.
         Checklist (mark each pass/fail): (a) every Implementation-Map file
         still exists and matches; (b) the mermaid diagram still reflects the
         actual flow; (c) all intra-doc links resolve; (d) documented config
         matches real config/.env; (e) (user) no developer leakage present;
         (f) documented steps still work IN ORDER against current code;
         (g) (user) UI labels in the doc match the current UI.
       LEAKAGE (L, user only) = replace developer content with user-facing
         equivalents using the standard mapping (src/... -> the page/feature
         name; npm install / git clone -> the production URL or remove;
         localhost/staging URL -> the production URL or remove; ClassName ->
         the system it implements; handleSubmit() -> "clicking Submit").
       Then tick this item -> [x] in _UpdateQueue.md and bump counters.
       Acceptance: action applied, queue + manifest updated, links valid.'
WIRING RULES — obey exactly to stay deadlock-free:
  - Parent EVERY spawned bead to $SELF (so the finalize fan-out gate counts it).
  - No dependency from any fix bead to the finalize bead.
  - No blocks edges between fix beads — they are independent.
  - Do NOT create your own finalize/rollup bead; one already exists and waits.
  - Spawn AT LEAST ONE child. If there is genuinely no drift, spawn a single
    VERIFY bead ("confirm docs current, no drift found") so the gate resolves.
  - Do NOT close any bead — the LLM judges close. (bead-chain blocks bd close.)

Stop after writing the queue + spawning. Summarize findings and how many fix
beads you spawned.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: finalize maintenance (archive queue to audit log)
steps.description:
FINALIZE the {{audience}} maintenance pass. You become ready only once 'audit'
and EVERY fix bead it spawned are closed (fan-out gate).

DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.
1. Confirm every item in DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md is checked [x]; fix counters.
2. Append a completion summary (updates made / new / removed / verified) to
   DOCS_DIR/_AuditLog.md, then delete DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md.
3. Verify _Manifest.md, all index files, and intra-doc links are consistent
   after the additions/removals.
READ-ONLY source. Output only under DOCS_DIR. Do NOT close any bead yourself.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: validate maintained docs against the rubric
steps.description:
VALIDATE the {{audience}} documentation set after the maintenance pass.
POUR-GATE (bdboard-4iud): this step declares depends_on: finalize, so it stays
BLOCKED until 'finalize' closes - and finalize is itself gated on
children-of(audit), i.e. every spawned fix bead. The validate gate is therefore
baked into the formula: it cannot start until the whole poured maintenance pass
is complete, with NO manual 'bd dep add' after the pour. (It depends on the
finalize LEAF task, not the epic, per the bd rule that tasks cannot block epics.)

DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.
RUBRIC = DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md (the authoring contract) plus
DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md and the dated DOCS_DIR/_AuditLog.md entry. Score the
maintained docs and record residual deltas in this bead's notes:
1. QUEUE CLEARED: _UpdateQueue.md is gone and its findings are reflected in the
   docs; _AuditLog.md carries the completion summary.
2. MANIFEST PARITY: every _Manifest.md item is checked [x], the file exists at
   its PascalCase path, and counters reconcile (after any NEW/REMOVE).
3. TEMPLATE FIDELITY: touched docs still carry EVERY section + table their
   _FlowDocGuide.md template prescribes - no placeholders, real values.
4. DRIFT CLOSED: the audit findings are actually fixed (Implementation Maps,
   config tables, flows/diagrams match current source); re-run the VERIFY
   checklist spot-wise.
5. LINKS: intra-doc links resolve and related docs link bidirectionally.
6. (user edition only) LEAKAGE: no source paths, code/shell snippets,
   class/function names, or localhost/dev/staging URLs remain.
Produce a scorecard (per-gate PASS/FAIL + an overall fidelity %) in this bead's
notes. For any residual gap, file one fix bead per finding (Bug Discovery
Protocol) rather than fixing inline. READ-ONLY source. Do NOT close any bead
yourself - the LLM judges close it.
